name: Siam (1782–1817)

colors.symbolism: the chakra, emblem of the ruling Chakri dynasty

description: A red field bearing a white chakra (Chakri wheel) at the centre.
history: Under Rama I a white chakra — the wheel emblem of the new Chakri dynasty — was placed on the plain red flag, used for naval and royal vessels from 1782 until a white elephant was added inside the wheel in 1817.
